title: Oerstedella pseudowallisii
intro: Oerstedella pseudowallisii, also known as Wallis's orchid or "Queen of the Andes", is a type of orchid native to the high-altitude regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Oerstedella pseudowallisii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Oerstedella pseudowallisii is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and infusions used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
